sourceMovie.title: Paprika
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Paprika (2006) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Satoshi Kon's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Neon Genesis Evangelion: The End of Evangelion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Paprika clicked for you, Neon Genesis Evangelion: The End of Evangelion (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Paprika?
faq.answer: Try Neon Genesis Evangelion: The End of Evangelion, Memories, Steins;Gate: The Movie - Load Region of Déjà Vu—each shares DNA with Paprika while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Paprika?
faq.answer: Paprika (2006) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Paprika worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
